title: feat: make playground and websim embed-friendly
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Makes the Playground and Websim apps render correctly when embedded in the pollinations.ai `/play` iframe — the reference implementation of the self-contained embeddable-app pattern.

- Show the BYOP auth menu when embedded (previously hidden via `hiddenWhenEmbedded`); each app keeps its own auth on its own origin.
- Hide the app title when embedded — the host tab selector already names the app; the subtitle stays.
- Tighten top spacing in embed mode (`pt-2`, menu `top-2`); standalone unchanged.

CatGPT gets the same treatment in #11614.

gist.headline: Perfecting the /play pen
gist.blurb: Huge thanks to ElliotEtag for making Playground and Websim look right at home when embedded. You can now bring your own pollen (BYOP) straight from the iframe!
gist.summary: ElliotEtag updated the Playground and Websim apps to render correctly when embedded in the /play iframe by enabling the BYOP auth menu, hiding redundant titles, and optimizing top spacing.
gist.impact: Users can now access their BYOP auth settings and enjoy a cleaner, tighter UI when using Playground and Websim directly within the pollinations.ai iframe.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a builder/decorator, fitting a smaller screen or puzzle piece into a larger frame, representing the seamless embedding of apps. A retro monitor displaying a playground.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat and tool belt, carefully sliding a glowing digital puzzle piece into a retro CRT monitor. The monitor screen glows with a soft lime green (#ecf874) light, displaying a tiny digital playground. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background with chunky retro sprites. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
